title: OpenAI Introduces Teen Safety Policies for AI Developers
summary: OpenAI has released prompt-based safety policies for developers using gpt-oss-safeguard to moderate age-specific risks in AI systems. These guidelines aim to help developers create safer AI experiences for teenage users.
body:
OpenAI has unveiled new safety policies designed to help developers build safer AI experiences for teenagers. The policies are integrated into gpt-oss-safeguard, a tool that helps moderate age-specific risks in AI systems. These guidelines provide developers with prompt-based strategies to ensure that AI interactions with teens are safe and appropriate.

The new policies address a growing need for age-specific safeguards as AI systems become more prevalent in educational and social applications for younger users. By offering developers clear guidelines, OpenAI aims to mitigate risks such as exposure to inappropriate content, privacy concerns, and potential manipulation. This move is part of a broader effort to make AI technology more inclusive and safe for all age groups.

The introduction of these policies is likely to influence how other AI developers approach safety measures for younger users. As AI continues to integrate into various aspects of education and social interaction, the need for robust safety protocols will only grow. Developers will now have a framework to build upon, potentially setting a new standard for teen safety in AI applications.
twitterThread:
🚨 OpenAI just dropped new safety policies for AI and teens. Here's why it matters.

Developers using gpt-oss-safeguard now have prompt-based guidelines to moderate age-specific risks. Think: inappropriate content, privacy, manipulation.

Here's why this matters: AI is everywhere. Schools, social apps, even home devices. Teens are using it. But safety measures? Not so much.

The wild part: These policies could set a new standard. Other AI devs will have to follow. Or risk being left behind.

This changes everything for AI in education. Imagine safer chatbots, tutors, and social platforms. Finally.

Full breakdown → https://ainformed.dev
standaloneTweet: OpenAI's new teen safety policies for AI developers could redefine how we protect young users. Here's what you need to know: https://ainformed.dev/articles/openai-introduces-teen-safety-policies-for-ai-developers
